data.title: Booking Holdings Posts Q1 Earnings Beat, Lowers Outlook On Middle East Conflict
data.description: Booking Holdings Inc (NASDAQ: BKNG) saw a decline in shares following its first-quarter results. Analyst Tom White of DA Davidson maintained a Buy rating and lowered the price target from $240 to $230. BTIG analyst Jake Fuller also reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $250. According to DA Davidson, Booking's results were solid despite challenges like the Iran war at the end of the quarter. The company exceeded expectations in adjusted EBITDA and earnings. However, room nights and gross bookings were below guidance. Management's forecast for the second quarter indicates ongoing challenges related to the conflict. For more details, refer to Benzinga.com.

data.title: Peter H. Tracy Introduces the PHTLive Grant for Creative Minds to Support Undergraduate Innovators in Music and Storytelling
data.description: Peter H. Tracy has introduced the PHTLive Grant for Creative Minds in Boca Raton, Florida on April 29, 2026. This initiative focuses on promoting creative expression, storytelling, and student innovation. The grant aims to support individuals who exhibit exceptional talent in these areas.

data.title: Orlando Delivers a Summer Filled with Headline Concerts, Vibrant Festivals and New Attractions
data.description: Orlando is set to provide an exciting summer experience in 2026, featuring a variety of events. Visitors can look forward to headline-worthy concerts, nighttime water park takeovers, character-driven experiences, food and music festivals, and international sporting events. This summer promises unforgettable moments for tourists with a wide range of activities to choose from.

data.title: Immune Cells Rewire Themselves to Keep Battling Tumors in Nutrient Poor Environments
data.description: Researchers from the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia have made a breakthrough discovery, revealing the mechanism through which killer T cells, specifically CD8+ cells, function in immunotherapy. This finding, made public on April 29, 2026, could pave the way for significant advancements in immunotherapy. The hope is that this study will lead to enhancements in treatment efficacy.

data.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $76,000 But These 3 Billionaires Stay Bullish
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is increasingly being touted by major investors as a macro-driven asset poised for a prolonged bullish cycle. In a podcast on Apr. 29, Anthony Pompliano pointed out growing optimism among top macro and crypto investors, who view Bitcoin as entering a structurally bullish phase rather than a speculative surge. The positive outlook hinges on four main factors: expanding global liquidity, increasing institutional involvement, growing government interest, and ongoing inflation worries. These elements are collectively perceived as laying the groundwork for a sustained upward trajectory in Bitcoin's value. BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes also highlighted that the primary driver for Bitcoin's next significant movement will likely be the expansion of "digital credit" and global liquidity cycles, rather than retail speculation. Hayes emphasized the importance of liquidity in shaping Bitcoin's future market dynamics.

data.title: Shareholder Alert: Ademi LLP investigates whether KalVista Pharmaceuticals Inc. is obtaining a Fair Price for Public Shareholders
data.description: An investigation is being conducted by Ademi LLP in Milwaukee on April 29, 2026, regarding potential breaches of fiduciary duty and other legal violations by KalVista (NASDAQ: KALV) related to its recent deal with Chiesi Group. To participate in the investigation and gain further information, visit the link provided.

data.title: Air Canada Welcomes Positive Ratification Votes by its Flight Operations Crew Schedulers and its In-Flight Crew Schedulers on New Four-Year Collective Agreements
data.description: In Montreal on April 29, 2026, Air Canada expressed satisfaction with the outcome of a vote conducted among its Flight Operations Crew Schedulers and In-Flight Crew Schedulers, who are members of Unifor, signaling their acceptance of a fresh collective agreement with the airline. The four-year collective agreements will remain valid until May 22, 2029.

data.title: Squads Raises $18M to Build Business Finance on Stablecoin Infrastructure
data.description: Today, in New York, it was revealed that Squads has secured $18 million in a strategic round of funding. The round was spearheaded by Solana Ventures, and also saw the participation of Coinbase Ventures, Haun Ventures, L1D, Collab+Currency, Electric Capital, Placeholder, Jump Crypto, and Robot Ventures. This latest funding round has brought the total funding for Squads to a significant amount.

data.title: Paul Tudor Jones Calls Bitcoin 'Unequivocally The Best Inflation Hedge'
data.description: Prominent macro trader Paul Tudor Jones declared Bitcoin as the ultimate inflation hedge, surpassing gold due to its fixed supply cap of 21 million. In a recent podcast, the founder and CIO of Tudor Investment Corp emphasized that while gold's supply increases annually by a few percent, Bitcoin has a limited amount available for mining. Presently, less than 1 million BTC remain out of the total 21 million. Jones encouraged investing in Bitcoin to safeguard against central bank policies. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
